project_overview.project_name: Strengthen Ethiopia's Adaptive Safety Net Project (SEASN)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To expand geographic coverage and enhance service delivery of Ethiopia's adaptive rural safety net to improve the well-being of extremely poor and vulnerable households in drought-prone communities, and in case of an Eligible Early Response Financing Event respond promptly and effectively to it
project_overview.total_financing: US$512.5 million (IDA Credit: US$200 million, IDA Grant: US$312.5 million including US$12.5 million from Crisis Response Window)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 8 million direct beneficiaries from 2.5 million extremely poor rural households in drought-prone areas

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - operates in drought-prone and climate-vulnerable areas with some conflict-affected regions like Somali and Afar

key_components.description: Delivery of safety net operations including public works, mother and child package, safety net transfers, and complementary livelihood services

key_components.component_name: Improve Shock Responsiveness of the Rural Safety Net
key_components.description: Geographic expansion to drought-prone woredas, shock responsive systems, and contingent early response financing

key_components.component_name: Systems, Capacity Development, and Program Management Support
key_components.description: Systems development including MIS rollout, capacity building, and program management

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture through Food Security Coordination Directorate (FSCD)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Multi-agency coordination involving Ministry of Finance, Ministry of Labor and Social Affairs, National Disaster Risk Management Commission, with donor coordination through PSNP Donor Working Group
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, using government Channel One fund flow mechanism
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Biannual impact evaluations, quarterly spot-checks, regular program monitoring, and joint review and implementation support missions

identified_risks.risk_description: Political turmoil, inter-ethnic conflict, weakened upward accountability of subnational governments, upcoming elections
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close monitoring of federal-regional-woreda relations, maintaining strong field presence, flexible implementation arrangements

identified_risks.risk_description: Food management system inadequacies, low capacity in lowland regions, commodity tracking and reporting challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strengthening information systems, increasing cash transfers share, capacity building in lowlands, expanding electronic payment systems

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential adverse impacts from public works if poorly designed, social exclusion, child labor, gender-based violence risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Management Framework implementation, grievance redress mechanisms, labor management procedures, community engagement

identified_risks.risk_description: High debt distress risk, foreign exchange shortages, inflation affecting benefit adequacy
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government commitment to increase financing share, automatic benefit adjustment using CPI, IMF program indicative spending targets

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1 Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks
ESS3 Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Kebele Appeals Committees at community level, enhanced with youth facilitators and regional focal persons
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: 50% women participation targets, childcare centers, extended maternity exemptions, gender-sensitive livelihood training, electronic account ownership promotion

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Program includes protocols for internally displaced beneficiaries, screening tools for displacement risks, operates in conflict-affected lowland regions with adapted approaches
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Operates in drought-prone areas with high climate vulnerability, addresses environmental degradation as underlying cause of poverty and conflict, builds resilience through watershed management
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Implementation affected by periodic political unrest and inter-ethnic conflict, security factors limit program expansion in some areas like Somali region
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - includes provisions for internally displaced PSNP beneficiaries, maintains entitlements for displaced persons returning within one year, protocols for hosting displaced families
